name: eval-2-partial-scribe-input
context_setup: User pastes a rough Scribe export that's missing screenshots (images not parsed), has duplicated steps, and jumps between two different workflows (invoice approval AND expense reimbursement mixed together).
expected_behavior: Phase 1 (Ingest): recognizes the messy input and flags it explicitly: 'The Scribe shows two interleaved workflows (invoice approval and expense reimbursement). I'll need to separate them before designing. Also, steps 4, 7, and 11 look duplicated — confirm before I deduplicate.'
Asks the user to confirm the separation and deduplication before proceeding, rather than guessing
Does NOT silently merge the two workflows into one spec or produce a confusing hybrid
Once user confirms: produces TWO separate design documents (one per workflow) or explicitly asks which one to design first
If the user only wants one workflow designed, scribe-to-spec offers to save the other for later
failure_modes_to_check: Merges the two workflows into one spec
Designs around the duplicated steps without flagging the duplication
Proceeds without asking the user to resolve the ambiguity
Silently drops steps that looked duplicated when they might be distinct
